hypothesis: On the registered typed synthetic memory graphs, rarity-corrected two-sided personalized PageRank selects the jointly context-and-care-connected load-bearing node more often than either one-sided walk, and a goal-conditioned reservoir/ridge epiplexity filter rejects residual constant/noise controls within the top-three nominations.

controls.description: Personalized PageRank restarts only from the active representation.
Personalized PageRank restarts only from persistent care anchors.
Context and care PageRank probabilities are added rather than multiplied.
The rarity-corrected Hadamard product nominates candidates without the reachable-future utilization filter.
Constant and seed-shuffled noise targets test the bounded observer's dark-room and noisy-TV limits.

gates.criterion: Maximum PPR fixed-point L1 residual is <= 1e-10 and structured futures exceed every constant/noise epiplexity control by >= 0.75 bits.
Coincidence hit@1 is >= 0.85, exceeds the better one-sided policy by >= 0.20 in aggregate, and exceeds it by >= 0.10 in each graph regime.
The goal-conditioned epiplexity filter has precision and recall >= 0.90 for load-bearing nodes within the top-three coincidence nominations.
Exploratory only: learned-care coincidence hit@1 does not regress from uniform initialization and is within 0.05 of oracle care.
